message:
chore: update Cargo.lock, beads tracker, AGENTS.md rch guidance, README.md troubleshooting, and testkit deps

- Cargo.lock: dependency version bumps from workspace changes.
- .beads/issues.jsonl: issue tracker updates.
- AGENTS.md: add rch failure-class guidance distinguishing remote-
  command success with retrieval failure, worker-runtime toolchain
  drift, and local fail-open behavior in multi-agent sessions.
- README.md: add troubleshooting row for remote toolchain drift
  causing unexpected local fallback, and expand rch retrieval-failure
  documentation.
- fcp-testkit/Cargo.toml: add fcp-conformance as a dependency for
  conformance vector access in test infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-es2n","title":"[IDEA-23.2] Unit tests for lifecycle transitions","description":"## Coverage
- Valid transition sequence.
- Failed health → rollback.
- Repeated crashes → quarantine state.

## Logging
- Structured logs with connector_id + version.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-esfoi","title":"[BUILD] fcp-host doctor_handler generic lifetime bound (E0310)","description":"Fix workspace build failure in crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs where doctor_handler generic R: ConnectorRegistry requires an explicit 'static lifetime bound. Validate with rch-offloaded cargo check commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:42:01.047933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:54.611622Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:54.611590Z","close_reason":"Added 'static bound to doctor_handler generic; workspace check passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q","title":"[FCP3/P7.1] Maintain the final quarantine scoreboard and exit gates for every temporary seam","description":"Background: by the time cutover approaches, every temporary seam should already be enumerated. The final pass needs a stricter scoreboard that ties each remaining seam to a concrete deletion gate and proof dependency.

Scope: maintain the final quarantine scoreboard for broad exports, compatibility seams, host first only teaching paths, transitional adapters, and runtime holdouts. Each item needs a reason it still exists, the proof required before removal, the exact bead or acceptance artifact that will clear it, the unit or integration or end to end coverage that guards the replacement path, the transcript or log bundle that demonstrates the replacement behavior, and the user visible workflow that must remain intact when the seam disappears.

Acceptance: there is no ambiguous transition residue; every remaining temporary seam is explicitly justified, tied to exact proof gates and concrete verification artifacts, and scheduled for removal without guessing about user impact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:08.950097Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:48:51.333068Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-7","scorecard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:54.426219Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-mm3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:53.658670Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","title":"[FCP3/P3.4] Establish durable object lifecycle, repair, and garbage collection invariants","description":"Background: durable objects are only useful if their lifecycle is explicit. Mesh placement and failover depend on being able to reason about object creation, mutation, compaction, replay, repair, and garbage collection without guessing.

Scope: define invariants for object lifecycle, repair, and garbage collection across the durable plane, including when checkpoint chains are authoritative, how revoked or superseded objects remain auditable, and how repair workflows recover canonical truth after partial failure. Add deterministic lifecycle snapshots, crate local unit tests, repair integration tests, failure oriented end to end test scripts, repair transcripts, and garbage collection reason logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can assert directly.

Acceptance: the durable plane has explicit lifecycle, repair, and garbage collection semantics that downstream mesh and operator flows can rely on, with auditable artifacts, tests, and logs that make repair and retention regressions diagnosable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureOriole","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.690966Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-30T22:29:12.687517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","gc","lifecycle","mesh-first","phase-3","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:26.234448Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:26.758398Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2101,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"AzureOriole","text":"Completed GC transcript sub-slice for durable lifecycle bead.

Changes:
- Added public GC audit types in crates/fcp-store/src/gc.rs: GcDecisionAction, GcReasonCode, GcDecision, GcTranscript, GcRunReport.
- Added collect_with_transcript() and collect_and_prune_symbols_with_transcript() while preserving existing aggregate APIs.
- Sweep planning now emits deterministic per-object decisions in sorted ObjectId order.
- Re-exported transcript types from crates/fcp-store/src/lib.rs.
- Added unit coverage in crates/fcp-store/src/gc.rs for deterministic reasons, lease-policy collection, reason-code wire stability, and run-report serde.
- Added downstream-facing integration coverage in crates/fcp-store/tests/no_mock_integration.rs for collect_with_transcript() and GcRunReport serde.

Verification:
- Main workspace rch is still not trustworthy for this repo path. Fresh attempt: rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-store --lib -> RCH-E326 fail-open on vmi1153651, local fallback interrupted immediately.
- Compliant reduced-workspace verification passed via rch in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors_rch_verify_ndoro_20260328:
  * cargo check -p fcp-store --lib
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_collect_with_transcript -- --nocapture
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_prunes_symbol_store -- --nocapture
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_run_report_serde_roundtrip -- --nocapture
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_reason_code_wire_format_is_stable -- --nocapture
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_collect_with_transcript_exposes_reason_log --test no_mock_integration -- --nocapture
  * cargo test -p fcp-store gc_run_report_serde --test no_mock_integration -- --nocapture

Bead remains open: lifecycle snapshot and repair-transcript portions still remain.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:54:35Z"},{"id":2111,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"2026-03-29 fresh-eyes review follow-on of the store GC/lifecycle slice found a concrete root-validation bug in crates/fcp-store/src/gc.rs. The collector already aborted on missing roots, but it silently ignored roots that existed in the object store with the wrong zone_id. That meant a mis-bound zone checkpoint or pinned root could be treated as if the zone had no root, allowing an accidental sweep of unrelated local objects. I tightened collect_internal() so any configured GC root that resolves to a foreign-zone object now returns GcError::InvalidRoot instead of being skipped, and I added gc_foreign_checkpoint_root_returns_invalid_root_without_sweeping to lock the behavior in. Verification: rustfmt passed; compliant rch cargo verification attempt again stalled in the known 188-root workspace sync loop before any remote cargo execution started, so I interrupted it rather than allow local fail-open cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-29T22:50:31Z"},{"id":2113,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"2026-03-29 follow-up fresh-eyes pass on my own gc.rs edits found one consistency bug in the debug/test helper: would_collect() still silently ignored invalid roots even though collect() now aborts on them. That meant the helper could claim an object would be collected in scenarios where the real collector would refuse to run. I tightened would_collect() to stay conservative when the root set is missing or foreign-zone and added regressions for both cases. rustfmt passed. A narrow rch exec cargo test attempt for the new helper regression again stalled in the repo-scale 188-root sync loop before remote cargo execution started, so I interrupted it without allowing local fallback cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-29T22:53:27Z"},{"id":2114,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up in crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs: pending repair queue entries were append-only snapshots keyed by object_id. Repeated evaluate_zone() runs could leave stale requests in place, so stricter policy / worse coverage was not refreshed and recovered objects could still be repaired unnecessarily. Fixed by collapsing duplicates into the latest request, pruning stale per-zone entries during evaluation, and adding regressions for policy-refresh and recovery-removal behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:01:52Z"},{"id":2124,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-29 NobleFern: picked up the remaining repair-transcript/report portion of this lifecycle bead on a non-overlapping fcp-store slice. Added public repair evaluation report types and stable reason/action enums in crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs, added evaluate_zone_with_report(...) with deterministic per-object queue decisions, re-exported the new API from crates/fcp-store/src/lib.rs, and added unit + store integration coverage for convergence / queue refresh / policy-removal behavior. Verification is in progress via crate-scoped rch exec from crates/fcp-store because repo-root rch sync is still too broad.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:20:05Z"},{"id":2127,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"RubyCreek","text":"RubyCreek taking the remaining deterministic lifecycle snapshot sub-slice after AGENTS/README/code archaeology. Planned narrow surface: crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs, crates/fcp-store/src/lib.rs, and focused store integration coverage. I am intentionally staying off the active gc.rs and repair.rs slices already advanced by AzureOriole/SageDuck/NobleFern. Goal: publish a deterministic zone/object lifecycle snapshot surface that downstream conformance can assert directly alongside the new GC and repair transcripts.","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:19:58Z"},{"id":2129,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"RubyCreek","text":"Completed the lifecycle snapshot sub-slice in fcp-store. Added lifecycle snapshot API/types/errors and exports in object_store.rs/lib.rs/error.rs, added focused lifecycle integration coverage in store_repair_integration.rs, and fixed the existing missing fcp-testkit dev-dependency in crates/fcp-store/Cargo.toml so snapshot unit tests compile. Validation via rch on reduced verification workspace: 1) cargo check -p fcp-store --lib, 2) cargo test -p fcp-store --test store_repair_integration lifecycle_snapshot_reflects_reachability_and_symbol_state -- --nocapture, 3) cargo test -p fcp-store snapshot_zone_lifecycle -- --nocapture. Agent Mail inbox/send remains intermittently blocked by the storage.sqlite3 database error, so progress is recorded here even though mail coordination is degraded.","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:57:51Z"},{"id":2134,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"RubyCreek","text":"Taking a downstream non-overlapping artifact-bundle slice while repair.rs remains active elsewhere: extending existing offline repair E2E coverage to assert lifecycle snapshot and GC transcript artifacts using the new fcp-store surfaces, without modifying repair.rs/gc.rs. Goal is to tighten ezkdk acceptance and smooth the handoff into si48n conformance/evidence work.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:15:01Z"},{"id":2136,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"RubyCreek","text":"Completed the downstream artifact-bundle slice for ezkdk in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/offline_repair_e2e.rs. Added an async offline-repair recovery test that captures a pre-repair lifecycle snapshot, deterministic repair plan, post-repair lifecycle snapshot, and GC transcript as one serialized evidence bundle, without touching the active repair.rs implementation. Verification via rch on the reduced workspace path /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors_rch_verify_ndoro_20260328: cargo check -p fcp-e2e --test offline_repair_e2e; cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test offline_repair_e2e offline_repair_artifact_bundle_captures_lifecycle_and_gc_evidence -- --nocapture. Local rustfmt + git diff --check passed for the edited test file. cargo fmt --check --all in the reduced workspace still reports unrelated pre-existing fcp-raptorq formatting drift, so I did not treat that as a blocker for this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:29:12Z"}]}

Verification via rch on the reduced workspace path /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors_rch_verify_ndoro_20260328: cargo check -p fcp-e2e --test offline_repair_e2e; cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test offline_repair_e2e offline_repair_artifact_bundle_captures_lifecycle_and_gc_evidence -- --nocapture. Local rustfmt + git diff --check passed for the edited test file. cargo fmt --check --all in the reduced workspace still reports unrelated pre-existing fcp-raptorq formatting drift, so I did not treat that as a blocker for this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:29:12Z"},{"id":2137,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"NobleFern","text":"Picking up a downstream non-overlapping ezkdk slice in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/offline_repair_e2e.rs. Goal: extend the existing offline repair artifact bundle to capture the deterministic repair evaluation report alongside lifecycle snapshots, pre/post plan state, and GC transcript so the bead has stronger failure-oriented durable evidence without touching the active fcp-store core implementation surfaces.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:54:47Z"},{"id":2138,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"NobleFern","text":"Fresh-eyes pass on the repair evaluation report path found and fixed a real queue ordering bug in crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs. Root cause: evaluate_zone_with_report() rebuilt the global queue as [other zones][current zone] without restoring global priority order, so refreshed high-priority requests could be starved behind lower-priority work from other zones. Fixed by centralizing deterministic queue sorting and adding a regression proving refreshed zone work still dequeues first.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:00:10Z"},{"id":2139,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"NobleFern","text":"Follow-up on the same repair-report slice: fixed a second queue-sync bug where evaluate_zone_with_report() would silently carry forward stale requests for objects that had disappeared from the symbol store as long as the policy entry still existed. That leaked dead repair work and produced no audit decision. I now prune those entries explicitly with the new stable reason code repair.object_missing and added a regression that queues an object, deletes it from the symbol store, and verifies the queue is emptied with a Remove decision.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:02:04Z"},{"id":2140,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","author":"NobleFern","text":"Deep review of the other-agent lifecycle/GC slices found two more concrete bugs. (1) In crates/fcp-store/src/gc.rs, collect_internal() still silently skipped foreign-zone roots even though missing roots already raised InvalidRoot and the lifecycle snapshot explicitly classifies foreign-zone roots. Fixed by rejecting any configured root whose header resolves outside the requested zone, and added gc_foreign_checkpoint_root_returns_invalid_root_without_sweeping. (2) In crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs, snapshot_zone_lifecycle() returned reconstructable = false for policy-bound objects with no symbol distribution, but left meets_placement_policy = None. That conflated no policy with policy present but currently unmet because symbol state is missing. Fixed by reporting Some(false) when placement exists but symbol distribution is absent, with regression coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:07:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","title":"TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests (Availability Tracking, Pre-staging, Coverage Metrics)","description":"# TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive unit tests for offline capability tracking from V2 spec section 21.

## Test Categories

### OfflineAccess Tests
- can_access() returns true when local_symbols >= k
- can_access() returns false when local_symbols < k
- coverage() calculates correct ratio (local_symbols / k)
- Edge cases: k=0, local_symbols=0, overflow

### OfflineCapability Tests
- Accessible map tracks multiple objects correctly
- Object removal updates capability state
- Query by zone returns correct subset
- Memory efficient for large object counts

### Pre-staging Tests
- Access pattern tracking updates correctly
- Frequent objects prioritized for pre-staging
- Pre-staging respects device storage limits
- Stale access patterns decay appropriately
- Pre-staging triggers before predicted access

### Integration with Stores
- Symbol store updates reflect in OfflineAccess
- Symbol eviction updates can_access() correctly
- Repair loop improves coverage metrics

## Golden Vectors
- Location: tests/golden/offline_capability/
- Include: OfflineAccess serialization, coverage calculations

## Logging Requirements (per 1n78.35)
All tests emit structured JSON:
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test": "can_access_sufficient_symbols",
  "object_id": "...",
  "local_symbols": 60,
  "k": 50,
  "expected_can_access": true,
  "result": "pass|fail"
}

## Fuzz Targets
- OfflineAccess from arbitrary symbol counts
- Pre-staging priority calculation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All can_access/coverage calculations correct
- Pre-staging improves offline availability
- Integration with stores is consistent","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:41:38.187529965Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:42:07.112633034Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:42:07.112633034Z","close_reason":"26 comprehensive unit tests covering all requirements: OfflineAccess, OfflineCapability, AccessPatternTracker with edge cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f4sw","title":"[IDEA-25] RaptorQ Parameter Auto-Tuning + Benchmarks","description":"## Background
Symbol size and repair parameters strongly impact performance and MTU safety. Current defaults are static and may be suboptimal per platform/path.

## Goals
- Add auto-tuning for symbol size and repair symbol count.
- Provide benchmark suite to compare configurations.
- Emit recommended parameters for common MTUs (LAN/DERP).

## Non-goals
- Changing the canonical wire format; only tune parameters.

## Approach
- Add a bench harness in `fcp-raptorq` and `fcp-cli bench` integration.
- Record decode/encode time, memory, and overhead.
- Store recommended presets in config.

## Risks
- Overfitting to benchmarks; keep conservative defaults and allow override.

## Success Criteria
- Bench suite produces consistent metrics across runs.
- Recommended parameters reduce p99 latency without MTU regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Bench suite outputs p50/p95/p99 and memory usage JSON.
- Preset table covers LAN and DERP MTU defaults.
","notes":"All child tasks (flywheel_connectors-3c0g, flywheel_connectors-mapp, flywheel_connectors-3giu) completed; preparing to close epic.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add placement optimizer with explicit availability constraint; log objective + constraint.
- Add repair scheduler with queueing bound and a worst-case backlog test.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use prefix sums for availability queries.
- Priority queue for repair task scheduling.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f50","title":"fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting","description":"# fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable controlled Reddit participation:
- submit posts
- submit comments
- edit/delete own content

These are real-world side effects and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Submit post to a subreddit (text/link).
- Submit comment.
- Edit content.
- Delete content.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.post.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.comment.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.content.edit` (dangerous)

Policy guidance:
- Posting should often require ApprovalToken depending on zone policy.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Content safety:
  - outbound content must be treated as a “publication” action
  - receipts + audit events include subreddit + post/comment id
  - never log post bodies

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.post.submit`
  - `reddit.comment.submit`
  - `reddit.content.edit`
  - `reddit.content.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - request formation
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - error mapping (subreddit rules, forbidden)

## Acceptance criteria
- Write operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","title":"fcp-sdk: make schema validation limits and rate-limit retry-after env-overridable","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:29.318867Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.684405Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.684186Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:40.914223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1955,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILES:
- src/lib.rs: MAX_SCHEMA_ERRORS=5, DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES=256KB, DEFAULT_MAX_ARRAY_LEN=1000, DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH=32
- src/retry.rs: DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT_RETRY_AFTER=Duration::from_secs(30)

The schema validation limits are defensive bounds that prevent DoS via malicious payloads. They're unlikely to need tuning in most deployments but should still be overridable for edge cases (e.g., connectors that legitimately handle large arrays or deeply nested JSON).

The rate-limit retry-after of 30s is a fallback when the server doesn't send a Retry-After header. This is reasonable but should be connector-tunable since some APIs have known default rate-limit windows.

Steps:
1. Add env var support for each: FCP_SDK_MAX_SCHEMA_ERRORS, FCP_SDK_MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES, FCP_SDK_MAX_ARRAY_LEN, FCP_SDK_MAX_DEPTH, FCP_SDK_RATE_LIMIT_RETRY_AFTER_SECS
2. Document all env vars in module rustdoc
3. Keep existing defaults unchanged","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fgot","title":"[IDEA-01.1] SDK runtime kit: trait surface + config schema","description":"## Goal
Define the minimal trait boundary and config schema for the runtime kit so streaming/polling supervisors can be used by connectors with platform-specific state.

## Details
- Add `SupervisorConfig` (backoff, jitter, retry budget, cooldown, shutdown timeout, max_consecutive_failures).
- Define connector-facing trait hooks:
  - `StreamingSession` (resume token, sequence, heartbeat state)
  - `PollingCursor` (offset/seq + persistence hooks)
- Define explicit health transition rules (Healthy → Degraded → Unhealthy) and expose `HealthSnapshot` updates.

## Tests
- Unit tests for config defaults and validation (reject nonsensical values).
- Compile-only tests in `fcp-sdk` to ensure trait ergonomics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Traits are stable enough to be used by both Discord- and Telegram-like connectors.
- Config defaults align with study docs (1s base, 60s cap, jitter on).","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fnzgx","title":"[tooling] rch worker nightly drift triggers local fail-open after crate-scoped remote cargo","description":"Background: after the earlier RCH-E326 dependency-cycle work, crate-scoped offload from crates/fcp-kernel is now viable enough to reach remote cargo. On 2026-03-30, rch exec -- cargo check --lib from crates/fcp-kernel prepared 11 roots, finished remote sync in about 20.8 seconds, and started remote cargo on worker vmi1149989. That remote run then failed because the worker lacked toolchain nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, after which rch attempted to fail open locally. In shared multi-agent sessions this local fallback is forbidden because it can start unplanned local compilation storms. Scope: track this as a distinct tooling blocker so agents can separate it from the older path-cycle issue and choose safer verification paths. Acceptance: 1) the failure mode is explicitly tracked in beads, 2) future agents can distinguish worker-nightly drift plus local fail-open from repo metadata cycles, 3) next steps are clear whether the remedy belongs in worker fleet maintenance, rch worker selection, or repo-local guidance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:50.105649Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:50.105649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.

Full saved items lifecycle. Reddit at 22 ops, tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MossyShore","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:35.521843538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629752Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1094,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"MossyShore","text":"Implemented Airtable attachment download hardening. Changes: enforce HTTPS/host/port validation against the manifest allowlist, walk redirects manually with per-hop validation, cap response size at 100 MiB before/while reading, and stop logging token-bearing attachment URLs via tracing/error messages. Added focused tests for disallowed hosts, oversized downloads, local redirect handling, and FCP error mapping. Validation completed: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-check cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-test cargo test -p fcp-airtable --test integration -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-clippy cargo clippy -p fcp-airtable --all-targets -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide cargo check was attempted via rch but fell back locally and is still blocked/contended by concurrent cargo activity from other agents, so I am leaving the bead in_progress until that clears or someone explicitly accepts the package-scoped verification.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:50:36Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented attachments.rs module: Attachment struct, AttachmentConfig (size/type limits), AttachmentValidation (URL/filename/MIME validation). 110 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:51Z"}]}

@@ -707,6 +707,12 @@ rch queue                     # See active/waiting builds
 
 If rch or its workers are unavailable, it fails open — builds run locally as normal.
 
+Treat these `rch` outcomes as different failure classes:
+
+- **Remote command succeeded, artifact retrieval failed later**: if `rch` prints `Remote command finished: exit=0` and only then fails during artifact retrieval, the remote Cargo step already succeeded. Treat that as `rch` retrieval or stale-worker state, not a repo build failure.
+- **Remote command failed because the worker runtime drifted**: if remote stderr says the worker is missing the repo-pinned nightly from `rust-toolchain.toml` or otherwise cannot satisfy the requested toolchain, treat that as worker-image or worker-selection drift, not as a repo metadata-cycle regression.
+- **Local fail-open after remote failure**: in shared multi-agent sessions, do **not** let the unexpected local Cargo fallback continue. Preserve the remote stderr, inspect `rch status --json` or `rch workers capabilities --refresh --command 'cargo +<toolchain> check --lib'`, and route the fix toward worker maintenance, worker selection, or repo guidance rather than starting an unplanned local compilation storm.
+
 **Note for Codex/GPT-5.2:** Codex does not have the automatic PreToolUse hook, but you can (and should) still manually offload compute-intensive compilation commands using `rch exec -- <command>`. This avoids local resource contention when multiple agents are building simultaneously.
 
 ---

@@ -1443,6 +1443,8 @@ turn into compilation bottlenecks. `rch` fails open to local execution if the wo
 
 This repo carries both `.rchignore` and a project-level `.rch/config.toml` for `.beads` hygiene. In the current upstream `rch` source, retrieval-side filtering comes from `.rchignore`, while `.rch/config.toml` keeps the same excludes visible in effective config and applied on the upload side. Older installed `rch` releases can predate that retrieval-side fix, so post-success retrieval failures may still appear even when the repo configuration is correct. If `rch` prints `Remote command finished: exit=0` and only then fails during `Retrieving build artifacts...`, treat that as a tooling or worker-state problem rather than a Cargo failure; keep the retrieval stderr, then retry or continue the investigation in `rch` itself.
 
+Treat remote toolchain drift as a separate class of failure from both repo build errors and retrieval-only problems. If remote stderr says the selected worker is missing the toolchain pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml` and `rch` then attempts to fail open locally, the root cause is worker runtime drift or worker selection, not a Cargo metadata-cycle regression inside this repo. In shared multi-agent sessions, stop the unexpected local fallback, preserve the remote stderr, and use `rch status --json` plus `rch workers capabilities --refresh --command 'cargo +<toolchain> check --lib'` to decide whether the remedy belongs in worker fleet maintenance, worker selection, or repo-local guidance.
+
 ```bash
 # Build the default workspace members (core platform crates)
 rch exec -- cargo build --release
@@ -1796,6 +1798,7 @@ Honest about what FCP doesn't do yet:
 | Connector returns `NotConfigured` | `configure` not called before `invoke` | Call `fwc config schema <connector>` to see required params, then configure |
 | `cargo build` OOMs on macOS | Too many parallel codegen units | Set `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-build` to avoid Cargo lock contention; or use `rch exec -- cargo build` to offload to remote workers |
 | `rch exec -- cargo ...` ends with rsync `overflow ... .beads/recovery_*` / `recv_file_entry` / code 22 | The remote Cargo command may already have succeeded, but artifact retrieval is still traversing a worker-side `.beads` recovery tree | Read the remote-command status lines first. If `rch` reports a remote `exit=0`, treat the compile/test itself as successful and the failure as tooling state. This repo mirrors the critical excludes in both `.rchignore` and `.rch/config.toml`; if retrieval still overflows, investigate `rch` client version and stale worker state rather than assuming Cargo failed. |
+| `rch exec -- cargo ...` fails remotely because a worker lacks the repo-pinned nightly and then tries to continue locally | The selected worker runtime drifted from `rust-toolchain.toml`; this is a worker-image or worker-selection problem, not a repo dependency-cycle failure | Preserve the remote stderr, do not trust the local fail-open path in shared multi-agent sessions, inspect `rch status --json`, and probe worker capability with `rch workers capabilities --refresh --command 'cargo +<toolchain> check --lib'` before deciding whether to fix worker maintenance, worker routing, or repo guidance. |
 | Clippy fails with `fcp-async-core` errors | Pre-existing lints in async-core test code | These are upstream; connector code is clean. Run clippy on specific crates: `cargo clippy -p fcp-<crate>` |
 | OAuth token refresh fails | Token expired between materialize and use | For `credential_id` auth, the egress proxy handles refresh. For `access_token`, re-run configure with a fresh token |
 | SSE stream stops without error | Connection idle timeout | The Anthropic SSE parser has a 16 MiB buffer limit and proper CRLF handling. Check network/proxy timeout settings |
